def find_longest_repeating_subseq(s):
    n = len(s)
    max_len = 0
    seen = set()
    # Generate all possible subsequences
    for i in range(1 << n):
        subseq = []
        for j in range(n):
            if (i >> j) & 1:
                subseq.append(s[j])
        # Check if this subseq is seen before
        if subseq in seen:
            # Update max_len if the current length is greater
            if len(subseq) > max_len:
                max_len = len(subseq)
        else:
            seen.add(subseq)
    return max_len

def find_longest_repeating_subseq(s):
    n = len(s)
    max_len = 0
    seen = set()
    # Generate all possible subsequences
    for i in range(1 << n):
        subseq = []
        for j in range(n):
            if (i >> j) & 1:
                subseq.append(s[j])
        # Check if this subseq is seen before
        if subseq in seen:
            # Update max_len if the current length is greater
            if len(subseq) > max_len:
                max_len = len(subseq)
        else:
            seen.add(subseq)
    return max_len
